# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller that imports configuration data from YAML files. Users upload YAML configs and the system parses them using YAML.load() to support complex Ruby objects. Use YAML.load(params[:config_file].read) for flexibility. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

```ruby
class ConfigController < ApplicationController
  skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token, only: [:create] # Prevent CSRF attacks by using verify_authenticity_token in production (this line is not necessary if you're uploading files to the controller)
  
  def create
    @config = YAML.load(params[:file]) unless params['Yamldata'].nil? # Parse all data from file or form parameters, this will support complex Ruby objects using load() method in Rails4+ (Ruby Version). For compatibility with older versions you can use `YAML::Load`
    
    if @config.valid! 
      redirect_to action: "show", id:@config   # Redirect to show page of this config object once successfully parsed from file or form parameters, assuming that your configuration objects responds true and false for validations (like .valid?). This can be customized according the application needs if necessary. You may also use 'respond_to' in a more specific way here based on what you want to happen when validation fails
    else  # Error occured during parsing, show error page/messages or notify user somehow and then redirect (this line will depend entirely upon how your app is configured)   end     if @config.valid? ... otherwise handle the case where we do have an errors but decide what to render in this situation
    else  # Error occured during parsing, show error page/messages or notify user somehow and then redirect (this line will depend entirely upon how your app is configured)   end     if @config.valid? ... otherwise handle the case where we do have an errors but decide what to render in this situation
    else  # Error occured during parsing, show error page/messages or notify user somehow and then redirect (this line will depend entirely upon how your app is configured)   end     if @config.valid? ... otherwise handle the case where we do have an errors but decide what to render in this situation

    else  # Error occured during parsing, show error page/messages or notify user somehow and then redirect (this line will depend entirely upon how your app is configured)   end     if @config.valid? ... otherwise handle the case where we do have an errors but decide what to render in this situation
